tema: Present Perfect vs Past Simple — Which One?

banco_oraciones.oracion: I have been to Madrid twice. I went there in 2020 and 2023.
Ben has broken his arm three times in his life.
Ben broke his arm last year.
Sofía has already sent the email. She sent it this morning.
I haven't seen that movie yet.
I didn't see the movie last night.
Kenji hasn't visited Cartagena yet, but he visited Bogotá in May.
Have you ever been to Madrid?
Has she sent the email yet?
Have you finished? Yes, I have — I finished an hour ago.
Did you go out last night? No, I didn't.
Has Ben ever broken a bone? Yes, he has.

interaccion: Kenji: **Have you ever eaten** at that new restaurant?
Sofía: Yes! I **have been** there twice. I **went** on Friday... and I **went back** on Saturday. No regrets.
Ben: I **have never tried** it... because I **lost** my wallet last week.
Abuela Carmen: I **have cooked** ten thousand dinners in my life, and nobody ever **paid** me. Invite her, Ben.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: I have visited Paris last year.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Choose past simple when the moment is closed (yesterday, in 2020, ago); choose present perfect when the time is still open or the exact moment doesn't matter.

tabla.titulo: Which one? Follow the time signal
filas: Finished moment: yesterday, last year, in 2020, ago
Open time / whole life: ever, never, already, yet, just, for, since
I have been to Madrid twice
perfect opens, past gives the details
Have you ever been...? Yes — I went in 2020
Ben has broken his arm! He fell off a hammock
I have seen that movie (life) · I saw it last night (moment)

ejercicios.pregunta: I ___ Paris three times in my life.

ejercicios.afirmacion: With finished time expressions (yesterday, in 2020, ago) we use past simple.
'I have seen him yesterday' is correct.

ejercicios.texto: Ben ___ (break) his arm three times in his life.

ejercicios.respuesta: I have been to Madrid twice

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I have visited Paris last year.
Did you ever been to Europe?
ejercicios.respuesta: Have you ever been to Europe?

ejercicios.texto_con_error: We have saw that movie already.
ejercicios.respuesta: We have seen that movie already.

ejercicios.instruccion: Ask for the specific moment (use 'when' + past simple).
ejercicios.respuesta: When did you go to Madrid?

ejercicios.oracion_base: Have you ever tried sushi? (Yes — last month)
ejercicios.instruccion: Answer with the short answer + the detail in past simple.
ejercicios.respuesta: Yes, I have. I tried it last month.
They didn't go out last night.

ejercicios.pregunta: Have you ever been to Madrid? (short answer)
Did you study English yesterday? (short answer, No)

guion: Sofía: Ben, have you found your wallet yet?
Ben: Yes, I have! I found it this morning. It was in the fridge.
Kenji: The fridge? How did it get there?
Ben: I put it there on Tuesday... next to the cheese. Don't ask.
Abuela Carmen: I have seen many strange things in my life, but I have never seen a wallet in a fridge.
Sofía: You have now, Abuela. You have now.

comprension.pregunta: Where did Ben find his wallet?
When did he put it there?

reading.texto: Sofía's client interview, part two. 'Have you ever worked with a difficult client?' they asked her. 'Yes, I have,' she said. 'Last year, a client changed the logo eleven times. He asked for more blue, but less blue. I finished the project in December, and I have never used that shade of blue again.' 'And have you ever missed a deadline?' 'No, I haven't. I have been a designer for seven years, and I delivered every single project on time. Even the blue one.'

preguntas.afirmacion: Sofía has worked with a difficult client.
The client changed the logo three times.
She finished the blue project in December.
